status_detail: Electrolyzer construction well advanced. 9 of 12 PEM electrolyzer modules delivered by Siemens Energy as of late 2025. Civil works complete. Commissioning expected H2 2026. Supported by EU IPCEI and French State (Plan de Relance / France 2030).

data_management.narrative_summary: Normand'Hy is a 200 MW PEM electrolysis project developed by Air Liquide in the Port-Jérôme industrial basin of Normandy, France. The plant uses Siemens Energy PEM electrolyzer modules (12 units of approximately 16.7 MW each) manufactured at the Berlin gigafactory. It will produce up to 28,000 tonnes of renewable hydrogen per year, with 75% of output going to TotalEnergies' Gonfreville refinery and 25% to hydrogen refueling stations along the Seine Axis for mobility applications. The total investment exceeds €400 million, supported by EU IPCEI and French government funding. Construction began in 2023–2024, with full commissioning expected in the second half of 2026.

power.grid_connection_detail: Connected to RTE French high-voltage transmission grid at Port-Jérôme industrial site

offtake.offtaker_name: TotalEnergies Gonfreville Refinery (75%), HysetCo and Seine Axis HRS network (25%)

rag_metadata.text_for_embedding: Normand'Hy is a 200 MW PEM electrolysis project developed by Air Liquide in the Port-Jérôme industrial basin of Normandy, France. The plant uses Siemens Energy PEM electrolyzer modules and will produce up to 28,000 tonnes of renewable hydrogen per year, with 75% of output going to TotalEnergies' Gonfreville refinery and 25% to hydrogen refueling stations along the Seine Axis. Total investment exceeds €400 million with EU IPCEI and French government support. Construction began in 2023 with full commissioning expected in H2 2026. Under construction. PEM. 200MW. France. Europe. refinery + mobility offtake.
